skill.name: lint-fixer
skill.description: Runs project lint checks, helps fix lint and type errors, and verifies changes with follow-up lint and test commands.
skill.summary: Fix lint issues while preserving code behavior and project style.
skill.author: yamadashy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backtick command references as Ruby or shell execution, but SKILL.md contains documentation, not executable code. The weak cryptography alert at SKILL.md:3 is unsupported by the file content. Residual risk is low because the workflow asks an agent to run project npm scripts and auto-fix tools.

low_findings.title: Project Command Execution Guidance
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are Markdown code spans that document lint commands, not executable Ruby backticks. Following the skill can still run project-defined npm scripts and auto-fix tools, so users should review scripts in untrusted repositories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file shows Markdown inline code spans and numbered workflow steps, not a script or executable source file. The remaining risk is operational because npm scripts execute project-controlled commands when the user follows the instructions.

content.value_statement: Lint failures slow code review and hide type or style problems. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through lint checks, targeted fixes, and verification steps.
actual_capabilities: Runs npm run lint to collect formatting, lint, type, and secret detection results.
Explains failures by category, including style issues, type errors, and detected secrets.
Applies safe lint fixes while preserving existing code behavior.
Uses Biome and oxlint auto-fixes when project configuration supports them.
Repeats lint checks and recommends tests to confirm the final state.
limitations: It depends on the repository's npm scripts and lint configuration.
It cannot guarantee that auto-fix tools preserve every intended behavior.
Type errors usually need manual code changes and project context.
It should not be used blindly in untrusted repositories.

use_cases.title: Prepare a pull request
use_cases.description: Run lint checks, fix style errors, and verify that code is ready for review.

use_cases.title: Clean up generated changes
use_cases.description: Normalize formatting and resolve lint warnings after a coding agent edits files.

use_cases.title: Triage CI lint failures
use_cases.description: Group lint, type, and secret detection failures so the right fix is applied first.

prompt_templates.title: Run basic lint
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the lint fixer workflow for this repository and summarize any issues before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Fix safe style issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Run npm run lint, apply safe formatting and lint fixes, then rerun lint to confirm the result.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve lint and type errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full lint workflow, separate type errors from style errors, fix them without changing behavior, and verify with lint and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Audit CI failures
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the lint fixer workflow to investigate the current CI lint failure, explain root causes, apply minimal fixes, and report remaining risks.

output_examples.input: Fix lint failures after a TypeScript refactor.
output_examples.output: A concise report that groups formatting fixes, type fixes, verification results, and any remaining manual follow-up.

output_examples.input: Run lint before opening a pull request.
output: Lint command executed and errors reviewed by category.
Final lint and test status reported.

output_examples.output: A finding summary that identifies the affected file, explains why secretlint objected, and recommends safe remediation.

best_practices: Review package scripts before running this workflow in a new repository.
Inspect auto-fix changes before committing them.
Run tests after lint fixes that touch executable code.
anti_patterns: Do not silence lint rules without understanding the original failure.
Do not change behavior just to satisfy a style rule.
Do not run project scripts from untrusted code without review.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the agent to run npm lint and test commands from the repository.

faq.question: Can it fix formatting automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, when the repository configures Biome or oxlint auto-fix behavior.

faq.question: Will it fix TypeScript errors?
faq.answer: It can help diagnose and fix type errors, but these often need manual reasoning.

faq.question: Does it detect secrets?
faq.answer: It includes secretlint in the documented lint workflow when the project has that setup.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. Review npm scripts before running commands from code you do not trust.

faq.question: Does it support tools beyond npm?
faq.answer: The documented workflow is npm-based. Other package managers require adaptation.
